checks.a_analysis: The output content consists of three paragraphs defining the SSIDs and a table containing document metadata. All of these elements have a direct basis in the source document. No fabricated body text, headings, or conclusions were found.
The source document does not contain phone numbers, emails, or URLs. The output correctly reflects this absence and does not invent any contact details.

The source is a single-page document. The output covers the introductory text and the footer table. While significant text within the central diagram is missing from the structured JSON, the diagram itself is represented by an image element, so the logical section is not entirely missing, rather it is partially represented via an image placeholder.
The footer table in the source contains two rows of metadata. The output JSON faithfully reproduces these two rows, each containing three cells as seen in the source layout.

checks.check_name: Significant text omission within a section
checks.a_analysis: The output omits a substantial amount of critical technical text labels present in the central diagram. This includes server names (XIQ, Enterprise AD, NCID, Policy Servers), VLAN and Subnet details (Employee - 210, agency; NCGovRoam - 410, guest; NCGov Guest - 410, guest), SSID broadcast/hidden status (hidden, broadcast), and several functional labels (User Authentication Requests, OOB Management, Data Center). The missing text accounts for approximately 40% of the informative content of the page, which exceeds the 25% threshold for a MAJOR defect.
checks.b_evidence_source: VLANs & Subnets: Employee – 210, agency; NCGovRoam – 410, guest; NCGov Guest – 410, guest. SSIDs status: Employee – hidden; NCGovRoam - broadcast; NCGov Guest – broadcast. Servers: XIQ, Enterprise AD, NCID, Policy Servers (EDC & WDC). Labels: User Authentication Requests, OOB Management, Data Center.
checks.b_evidence_output: The text content for these technical labels is completely missing from the structured JSON text/paragraph elements. While a high-level summary is provided in the AI-generated image alt text, it omits all specific technical identifiers like VLAN IDs and SSID visibility settings.

checks.check_name: Minor text differences beyond acceptable reformatting
checks.a_analysis: The text successfully converted in the paragraphs and table matches the source accurately. Dates, project names, and department titles are correctly transcribed without changes to meaning.

checks.a_analysis: I have reviewed the output and compared it with the source PDF. Each block of text (the definitions at the top and the footer table data) appears exactly once in the JSON output, matching its appearance in the source. There is no evidence of duplicated paragraphs, sections, or list items.

The source document is a technical diagram and does not contain traditional text headings. The output identifies the document title 'WLAN Service – Current' in the metadata, which is appropriate. No heading tags (H1, H2, etc.) are used in the element structure, which is consistent with the flat layout of the original document where text blocks are descriptors or table entries rather than hierarchical headings.

The footer table in the source contains two rows. The first row has three columns (Date, Project, Created By). The second row has three columns (Department, Description, and an empty area containing a seal). The JSON output correctly identifies this as a table with 2 rows and 3 cells per row, preserving the logical grouping of labels and values within the cells. The empty cell in the second row correctly reflects the layout where the seal is placed in the original document.

The reading order in the JSON output follows the logical top-to-bottom flow of the source document: starting with the introductory service definitions, followed by the central architectural diagram (captured as an image), and concluding with the metadata table at the bottom. This accurately represents the intended reading sequence of the original document.

There are no list elements in the JSON output. The introductory text at the top of the source, while providing three definitions, consists of distinct paragraphs which are correctly represented as paragraph elements in the JSON. Other potential list-like structures within the diagram (e.g., SSIDs or VLANs & Subnets) are part of the graphical diagram and were not extracted as structured text, which is an extraction choice rather than a conversion error.
The source document does not contain any hyperlinks or URLs. Correspondingly, the JSON output contains no links. No fabrication of links occurred.

checks.a_analysis: The source PDF is primarily a visual network topology diagram that organizes information spatially using icons, arrows, and labels to show the relationship between users and network resources. The converted HTML completely fails to reproduce this layout. The core content of the document—the visual model—is removed, leaving only the introductory text and the footer table. This fundamentally changes the visual organization and flow of information, as the primary explanatory mechanism of the document is lost.
checks.b_evidence_source: A central network topology diagram connecting 'Guests' and 'State Employees' to various networks like 'Internet Access' and 'State Network' via an 'Access Point'.
checks.b_evidence_output: The HTML output omits the entire central diagram, showing only the text descriptions from the top and a 'Document Information' table at the bottom.

checks.a_analysis: The metadata table at the bottom of the source PDF (containing fields like Date, Project, and Department) is rendered as a 'Document Information' table in the HTML. The conversion is successful; the table has visible borders, distinct cells, and readable content that preserves the relationship between the labels and their values.

The document contains two critical visual elements that are missing or broken in the HTML output. First, the large, complex network diagram that occupies the center of the page is absent (represented only by a small, blurry green fragment that is unidentifiable). Second, the 'Great Seal of the State of North Carolina' located in the bottom right corner of the source PDF is entirely missing from the HTML rendering.
checks.b_evidence_source: 1) The central network diagram with icons for people, clouds, and hardware. 2) The Great Seal of the State of North Carolina in the bottom-right corner.
checks.b_evidence_output: 1) A blurry green gradient box with no recognizable features. 2) The seal is completely absent.
checks.b_evidence_location: 1) Center of page 1. 2) Bottom right of page 1.

checks.a_analysis: There is no evidence of visual content duplication in the HTML output. Each block of text and the metadata table appear only once, matching the content structure (minus the missing diagram) of the source.
